_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in using multiple search engines (e.g., Google, Yahoo, LexisNexis, DataStar) and tools in conducting open-source searches.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of data catalog platform capabilities, including automated metadata harvesting, lineage visualization, search and discovery, classification, and glossary linkage.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-K-011 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-S-006 (DQ, cosine: 0.472), DG-S-008 (DG, cosine: 0.432).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.7/10 (raw 0.571). Shared vocabulary: [search]. DCWF-only terms [conducting, datastar, engines, google, lexisnexis, multiple] vs WIDAI-only terms [automated, capabilities, catalog, classification, data, discovery] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.857 (P=0.850, R=0.864). Balanced P/R — the 17-word DCWF element and 19-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.5%). "Skill in using multiple search engines (e.g., Google, Yahoo, LexisNexis, DataSta" and "Knowledge of data catalog platform capabilities, including automated metadata ha" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.499 < STS 0.571 (gap -0.071). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [conducting, datastar, engines] and [automated, capabilities, catalog] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.9%. Shared tokens: [search]. DCWF-only: [conducting, datastar, engines, google, lexisnexis, multiple]. WIDAI-only: [automated, capabilities, catalog, classification, data, discovery].
